title: May 14, 2008: The day world No 1 Justine Henin suddenly quit professional tennis
description: Every day, Tennis Majors takes you back in time to relive a tennis event which happened on this specific day. On May 14, 2008, Justine Henin, then the world No 1, shocked the tennis world with the sudden announcement that she was to retire

title: Italian Open 2026 Day Eight Recap
description: Day Eight at the Foro Italico was defined by rain, late-night heroics, and some questions about the tournament schedule. Luciano Darderi produced another quality performance to reach his first ATP Masters 1000 semi-final, battling past a resilient Rafael Jodar in a three-hour, eight-minute war that didn’t finish until 2:02 a.m. While the Italian crowd was …

title: Darderi outlasts Jódar after smoke, rain and a 2 a.m. finish to reach first Masters 1000 semi-final
description: Luciano Darderi (No 18) became the eighth Italian in the Open Era to reach a Rome semi-final, outlasting Spanish teenager Rafael Jódar (No 32) 7-6(5), 5-7, 6-0. Darderi meets Casper Ruud in the semi-final.

title: Svitolina, Gritty As Ever, Rallies Past Rybakina in Rome
description: The Ukrainian is back in the semifinals in Rome for the first time since she won her second title in 2018.

title: Svitolina turns it around against Rybakina to reach first Rome semi-final since 2018, Swiatek next
description: Elina Svitolina (No 7) turned the Foro Italico quarter-final around against Elena Rybakina (No 2), winning 2-6, 6-4, 6-4. She now meets three-time Rome champion Iga Świątek for a place in the final.

title: Elina Svitolina stuns Elena Rybakina with performance in Rome
description: In a battle of top-10 players, Elina Svitolina turned the tables on Elena Rybakina in stunning fashion. What happens next?

title: Venus Williams to Play Roland Garros Doubles
description: At the age of 45, Venus Williams will make her Roland Garros doubles return.

title: Rain, a 6-1 reversal, and a Ruud reset: Norwegian into Rome semi-final just in time to boost confidence before Roland-Garros
description: Casper Ruud (No 13) reached a first Rome semi-final since 2023 with a rain-interrupted 6-1, 1-6, 6-2 win over Karen Khachanov (No 14). The clay-court Norwegian returns to the top 20 and arrives in semi-final form a fortnight before Roland-Garros – fourth Rome semi-final, 10th Masters 1000 semi-final, eight of them on dirt.

title: Ruud Repels Khachanov for Fourth Rome SF
description: Casper Ruud reached his 43rd Tour-level semifinal.

title: Lorenzo Musetti withdraws from Roland-Garros after rectus femoris injury confirmed in Rome
description: Medical tests on Lorenzo Musetti's left leg have confirmed a rectus femoris injury.

title: “More like a clay-court player”: Swiatek is rediscovering the version of her game that won three Roland-Garros titles
description: Iga Swiatek is into the Rome semi-finals, hasn't faced a break point in two days, and on Wednesday told reporters she is back to "playing more like a clay-court player." Roig is delivering.

title: Lorenzo Musetti Withdraws from Roland Garros
description: The 2025 Roland Garros semifinalist will miss his favorite major.

description: In a sport built on tennis parents who became inseparable from the story, Jannik Sinner is asking for the opposite. In Rome, he drew the line at his family.

title: Swiatek’s level surges before Roland-Garros as Pegula falls in straight sets
description: Iga Świątek (No 4) dismantled Jessica Pegula (No 7) 6-1, 6-2 in Rome without facing a single break point, reaching her first semi-final of 2026 and her fourth at the Foro Italico. The three-time Rome champion has dropped seven games in her last three matches — and Roland-Garros is two weeks away.

description: Every day, Tennis Majors takes you back in time to relive a tennis event which happened on this specific day.
